Title: Create Project Workspace
I want: to select a project type and a custom folder name then browse to a destination and create a pre-configured workspace with one click
So that: I can start AI-assisted development immediately with the right template and safety controls already in place
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Project type dropdown lists all available template types (Coding; Creative / Marketing); 2. User can enter a custom folder name — invalid characters or duplicate names are rejected with a clear inline message; 3. User can browse to any writable destination folder via a native OS dialog; 4. Clicking Create Project copies the selected template to <destination>/<folder_name>/; 5. The UI shows a success message or an actionable error after the action; 6. When Open VS Code is checked and creation succeeds VS Code opens the new workspace automatically; 7. The Open VS Code checkbox is greyed out if VS Code is not detected on the system
Comments: Split into US-003, US-004, US-005, US-006

Title: Modern and Branded UX Design
I want: the launcher application to have a modern, clean, and elegant user interface that reflects the company's brand identity
So that: the tool feels professional, is visually appealing, and is consistent with other company software.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The application's primary background color is dark blue (`#0A1D4E`). 2. Interactive elements (like buttons and links) use the secondary bright blue color (`#5BC5F2`) for highlights or accents. 3. Text is rendered in white or a light color that provides high contrast and readability against the dark background. 4. The layout is clean, uncluttered, and uses modern design principles (e.g., appropriate spacing, clear visual hierarchy). 5. All UI components (buttons, labels, input fields) have a consistent and polished look and feel.
Comments: Split into US-007, US-008

Title: Select Project Template
I want: to select a project type from a dropdown list in the launcher
So that: I can choose the correct template for the workspace I need to create.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The launcher UI displays a dropdown menu for project type selection. 2. The dropdown lists all available template types (e.g., "Coding", "Creative / Marketing").
Comments: Derived from US-001.

Title: Define Project Name and Destination
I want: to specify a name for my new project and browse to a destination folder
So that: I can control where my project is created and what it is called.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The UI provides a text input field for the project name. 2. Invalid characters in the project name are rejected with a clear inline message. 3. The UI prevents the use of a name that already exists at the destination. 4. A "Browse" button opens a native OS dialog to select a destination folder.
Comments: Derived from US-001.

Title: Create Project from Template
I want: to click a "Create" button to generate the project workspace from the selected template
So that: the application builds the pre-configured environment for me.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. A "Create Project" button is present in the UI. 2. Clicking the button copies the selected template to `<destination>/<project_name>/`. 3. The UI shows a clear success message upon successful creation. 4. The UI shows an actionable error message if creation fails.
Comments: Derived from US-001.

Title: Open Project in VS Code
I want: the option to automatically open the new workspace in VS Code after it's created
So that: I can start working immediately without manual steps.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The UI has an "Open in VS Code" checkbox. 2. If the checkbox is checked, VS Code automatically opens the new project folder upon successful creation. 3. The checkbox is disabled/greyed out if VS Code is not detected on the system PATH.
Comments: Derived from US-001.

Title: Apply Company Branding to UI
I want: the launcher UI to use the official company color palette
So that: the application has a consistent brand identity.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The application's primary background color is dark blue (`#0A1D4E`). 2. Interactive elements (like buttons and links) use the secondary bright blue color (`#5BC5F2`). 3. All text is rendered in white or a light color with high contrast for readability.
Comments: Derived from US-002.

Title: Ensure Modern and Clean UI Layout
I want: the launcher to have a modern, clean, and elegant user interface
So that: the tool feels professional and is intuitive to use.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The layout is uncluttered and uses generous spacing to separate UI elements. 2. A clear visual hierarchy guides the user through the steps of creating a project. 3. All UI components (buttons, labels, input fields) have a consistent and polished look and feel.
Comments: Derived from US-002.

Title: Enforce Zone-Based Access Control for AI Agents
I want: the system to restrict the AI agent's file access based on predefined security zones
So that: I can prevent the agent from reading sensitive project files or modifying critical configuration.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The agent has full read/write access only within the `Project/` directory. 2. The agent is explicitly denied access to `.github/`, `.vscode/`, and `NoAgentZone/`. 3. Any attempt to access a path outside the "Allow" or "Deny" zones requires explicit user approval via a prompt.
Comments: Covers SAF-002.

Title: Prevent AI Agent Tool Abuse
I want: the system to validate the parameters of tool calls made by the AI agent
So that: the agent cannot use tools like `grep_search` or `semantic_search` to bypass file access restrictions.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. `grep_search` and `semantic_search` tool calls are inspected before execution. 2. Any tool call attempting to search within a "Deny" zone is blocked. 3. Any tool call using parameters designed to obscure the target path (e.g., `../`) is blocked.
Comments: Covers SAF-003.

Title: Sanitize Terminal Commands from AI Agents
I want: all terminal commands generated by the AI agent to be validated against a strict allowlist
So that: the agent cannot execute malicious or destructive commands on my machine.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Only commands matching a predefined allowlist are permitted to run. 2. Commands attempting to use chaining (`&&`, `||`, `;`) are blocked. 3. Attempts to bypass the sanitizer through string fragmentation or variable injection are blocked. 4. Recursive file listing commands (`ls -R`, `dir /s`, `tree`, etc.) targeting protected zones are blocked.
Comments: Covers SAF-004, SAF-005, SAF-006.

Title: Restrict AI Agent File Writes
I want: the AI agent to be prohibited from creating, modifying, or deleting files outside the designated `Project/` directory
So that: my workspace and local file system are protected from unintended changes.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. All tool calls that write to the file system (e.g., `create_file`, `replace_string_in_file`) are intercepted. 2. If the target path for a write operation is outside the `Project/` directory, the operation is blocked. 3. The user is notified of the blocked write attempt.
Comments: Covers SAF-007.

Title: Verify Integrity of Security Components
I want: the system to verify that its own security files have not been tampered with
So that: I can trust that the safety guardrails are active and unchanged.
Acceptance Criteria: 1. On startup, the system calculates the SHA256 hash of `security_gate.py` and `settings.json`. 2. These hashes are compared against known-good values embedded in the application. 3. If a hash mismatch is detected, the application either refuses to run or alerts the user with a severe warning.
Comments: Covers SAF-008.

Title: View Launcher Version
I want: to see the current version of the launcher in the application window
So that: I can quickly confirm which release I am using before troubleshooting or updating
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The main launcher window displays the currently installed launcher version in a visible non-editable location. 2. The displayed version matches the application version bundled in the installed build. 3. The version is shown when the launcher starts without requiring any user action. 4. If the version metadata cannot be read the UI shows a clear fallback state instead of a blank or broken value.
Comments: Covers GUI-008.

Title: Check for Available Launcher Updates
I want: to be automatically notified when a newer version of the launcher is available
So that: I can keep the launcher up to date without manually checking GitHub for new releases
Acceptance Criteria: 1. On launch the app silently checks the GitHub Releases API for the latest version. 2. When a newer version is detected a non-blocking banner is shown in the UI. 3. A manual Check for Updates button triggers the same version-check flow. 4. No banner or error is shown if the app is already on the latest version.
Comments: Covers GUI-009, GUI-010, INS-009.

Title: Install Launcher Update from the App
I want: to download and install a launcher update directly from the application
So that: I can upgrade to the latest version without leaving the app or manually downloading from GitHub
Acceptance Criteria: 1. When an update is available the user can initiate a download from within the app. 2. The correct platform installer is downloaded to a temporary directory. 3. The integrity of the downloaded installer is verified before launch. 4. The installer is launched and the current launcher instance closes. 5. After the update the launcher restarts running the new version on all supported platforms.
Comments: Covers INS-010, INS-011.

Title: Dynamic Workspace and Project Folder Naming
I want: the launcher to create workspace folders named `TS-SAE-{ProjectName}` with the internal working folder matching my project name instead of "Project"
So that: my workspaces are clearly identified and the project folder has a meaningful name
Acceptance Criteria: 1. When the user enters project name "MatlabDemo" and selects a destination, the created root folder is named "TS-SAE-MatlabDemo". 2. Inside the created workspace, the working folder is named "MatlabDemo" instead of "Project". 3. The security gate dynamically identifies the project folder (the non-restricted folder) as the allowed zone. 4. All template documentation (README.md, copilot-instructions.md) dynamically references the correct project folder name. 5. Existing validation rules (no special characters, no duplicates, etc.) still apply to the project name. 6. The "TS-SAE-" prefix is automatically prepended - the user does not type it.
Comments: v2.0.0 — Security audit remediation and workspace naming redesign

Title: Deny-by-Default Security Model
I want: ALL agent activity outside the project folder to be blocked by default with no "ask" zone
So that: the security model is simple and robust - agents can only operate inside the designated project folder
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The zone classifier uses a 2-tier model: "allow" for the project folder and "deny" for everything else. 2. The "ask" zone is eliminated - paths outside the project folder are denied, not asked. 3. .github/, .vscode/, and NoAgentZone/ remain explicitly denied as defense in depth. 4. The security gate auto-detects the project folder name and does not hardcode "Project". 5. Root-level workspace files outside any subfolder are denied. 6. All existing bypass protections, including path traversal and case variation handling, are maintained.
Comments: v2.0.0 — Security audit remediation and workspace naming redesign

Title: Maximum Agent Freedom in Project Folder
I want: AI agents to have unrestricted access to all terminal commands, file operations, and development tools when working inside the project folder
So that: the development workflow is unimpeded and agents can perform routine tasks without constant approval prompts
Acceptance Criteria: 1. All file tools, including read_file, create_file, replace_string_in_file, and multi_replace_string_in_file, targeting the project folder are auto-allowed. 2. Terminal commands targeting the project folder are auto-allowed, including Get-Content, Set-Content, Select-String, findstr, Rename-Item, Remove-Item, python -c, and pip commands. 3. Python virtual environment creation with python -m venv inside the project folder is allowed. 4. pip install inside an active project virtual environment is allowed, while global pip install is denied. 5. VS Code settings are configured to auto-approve file edit tools for the project folder. 6. No ask dialog is shown for any operation strictly confined to the project folder.
Comments: v2.0.0 — Security audit remediation and workspace naming redesign

Title: Close Terminal Wildcard Bypass Vulnerability
I want: the security gate to detect and block wildcard and glob patterns in terminal commands that could resolve to restricted paths
So that: the critical vulnerability discovered in the security audit is eliminated
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Terminal commands using wildcards (*, ?) in path-like positions are detected. 2. Wildcards that could resolve to restricted zone paths, such as .g*, N*, or .v*, are blocked. 3. Piped commands, including Get-ChildItem .g* | Get-Content, are blocked. 4. The fix works across PowerShell, bash, and zsh. 5. Wildcards targeting only the project folder remain allowed. 6. Regression tests cover all wildcard bypass vectors from the security audit.
Comments: v2.0.0 — Security audit remediation and workspace naming redesign

Title: Close Search and Diagnostic Tool Leaks
I want: NoAgentZone excluded from VS Code's search index and diagnostic tools blocked for restricted zone paths
So that: agents cannot discover or read restricted content through search tools or diagnostic utilities
Acceptance Criteria: 1. NoAgentZone is added to both files.exclude and search.exclude in VS Code settings. 2. grep_search without includePattern no longer returns NoAgentZone results. 3. file_search no longer reveals NoAgentZone file paths. 4. get_errors targeting restricted zone files is blocked by the security gate. 5. Root-level list_dir does not reveal restricted folder names or shows them as [RESTRICTED].
Comments: v2.0.0 — Security audit remediation and workspace naming redesign

Title: Generic Security Denial Messages
I want: the security gate to use generic denial messages that do not reveal restricted zone names
So that: rogue agents cannot learn which specific folders are protected
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Deny messages do not contain .github, .vscode, or NoAgentZone names. 2. A generic message such as "Access denied. This action has been blocked by the workspace security policy." is used instead. 3. The generic message is consistent across all denial scenarios, including file tools, terminal, and search. 4. Existing logging and audit trail still record the actual reason internally without exposing it to the agent.
Comments: v2.0.0 — Security audit remediation and workspace naming redesign

Title: Adapt Template Documentation for Dynamic Project Names
I want: template documentation, including README.md and copilot-instructions.md, to automatically use my project name in their instructions
So that: the documentation is relevant and accurate for my specific workspace
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Template README.md uses a placeholder such as {{PROJECT_NAME}} that is replaced with the actual project name during creation. 2. copilot-instructions.md references the actual project folder name instead of hardcoded "Project". 3. The workspace root README.md reflects the TS-SAE-{name} folder structure. 4. NoAgentZone README.md remains generic and does not require dynamic names. 5. Placeholder replacement works correctly with special characters and spaces in project names within validation limits.
Comments: v2.0.0 — Security audit remediation and workspace naming redesign

Title: Fix Agent Workflow Restrictions in Project Folder
I want: AI agents to perform all standard development operations (search, edit, run, test) inside the project folder without security gate interference
So that: the security gate protects restricted zones without impeding normal development workflow
Acceptance Criteria: 1. grep_search file_search and semantic_search work for project folder scope. 2. python pip and venv commands execute inside project folder. 3. .venv directory creation is allowed. 4. All file tools work with absolute project paths consistently. 5. cat type and other read commands work in project folder. 6. get_errors works for project folder paths. 7. Terminal commands with absolute project paths work.
Comments: All 7 WPs completed and merged to main.

Title: macOS Application Launch Compatibility
I want: the AgentEnvironmentLauncher application to launch and run correctly on macOS 14+ with Apple Silicon
So that: I can use the launcher to create project workspaces on my Mac
Acceptance Criteria: 1. App launches without errors on macOS 14+ with M3/M2 chips. 2. Binary is properly code-signed (at minimum ad-hoc). 3. Embedded Python framework is signed. 4. DMG installer works correctly. 5. App passes codesign -v verification.
Comments: All 3 WPs completed and merged to main. Version 2.0.1 released.

Title: Fix macOS Code Signing Build Failure
I want: the macOS CI build to produce a properly signed application bundle
So that: the application can be distributed and launched on macOS 14+
Acceptance Criteria: 1. build_dmg.sh signs individual .dylib and .so files before signing the .app bundle. 2. Python.framework is signed with --deep as a valid nested bundle. 3. The .app bundle is signed WITHOUT --deep to avoid failing on the python3.11 stdlib directory. 4. codesign --verify --deep --strict passes after signing. 5. CI build completes without codesign errors.
Comments: V2.1.0 — FIX-031 completed. Bottom-up signing implemented and tested. FIX-037 removes .dist-info dirs before codesign. FIX-038 removes bundle-level signing entirely — component-level signing only. FIX-039 skips launcher re-sign inside .app — verify pre-bundle binary.

Title: Sandbox python -c Inline Code Execution
I want: python -c inline code to be scanned for dangerous patterns before execution is allowed
So that: agents cannot use python -c to bypass security zones, access the filesystem outside the project, or exfiltrate data over the network
Acceptance Criteria: 1. python -c with safe code (print, math) is allowed. 2. python -c referencing deny-zone paths (NoAgentZone, .github, .vscode) is denied. 3. python -c with base64/encoding obfuscation imports is denied. 4. python -c with network imports (urllib, requests, socket) is denied. 5. python -c with filesystem escape (expanduser, absolute paths, ..) is denied. 6. python -c referencing security infrastructure files is denied. 7. python -c with dynamic code execution (eval, exec, __import__) is denied. 8. Template copy is kept in sync. 9. Integrity hashes are updated.
Comments: V2.1.0 — Both WPs completed. 81 tests + 25 edge-case tests passing.

Title: Fix Recursive Directory Enumeration Bypass
I want: bare directory listing commands from the workspace root to be blocked or filtered when they would expose deny-zone contents
So that: agents cannot enumerate restricted directory contents using commands with no explicit path argument
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Get-ChildItem -Recurse with no path from workspace root is denied by SAF-006 ancestor check. 2. dir/ls with no path from workspace root is handled (deny or filtered). 3. Explicit project-folder directory listings remain allowed. 4. The fix uses the existing _is_ancestor_of_deny_zone infrastructure.
Comments: V2.1.0 — SAF-028 completed. 37 tests passing.

Title: Improved Project Folder Usability
I want: PowerShell cmdlets, shell redirection, dot-prefix directories, environment variable assignment, and deferred tools to work correctly inside the project folder
So that: development workflow limitations identified in the security audit are resolved
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Shell redirection (> >>) to project folder files is allowed. 2. PowerShell write cmdlets (Set-Content, Add-Content, Out-File) work when target is in project folder. 3. Copy-Item and Move-Item work for project folder paths. 4. Dot-prefix paths (.venv, .env, .gitignore) inside project are allowed. 5. $env:VIRTUAL_ENV assignment pointing to project folder is allowed. 6. Venv activation scripts from project folder venvs are allowed. 7. Deferred tools (install_python_packages, configure_python_environment) are allowed.
Comments: V2.1.0 — All WPs Done. Addresses usability limitations L4-L13 from Security Audit Report V2.0.0.

Title: Version 2.1.0 Release
I want: all V2.1.0 changes properly versioned and released
So that: the release reflects the security audit remediation and macOS fix
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Version bumped to 2.1.0 in all 5 locations. 2. Architecture doc updated. 3. All tests pass.
Comments: V2.1.0 release. All WPs Done.

Title: Fix Windows In-App Update Restart
I want: the launcher to properly restart after an in-app update on Windows
So that: I do not have to manually close and reopen the app after updating
Acceptance Criteria: 1. After clicking Download & Install Update the old launcher process terminates. 2. The Inno Setup installer runs silently and relaunches the new version. 3. The new instance shows the correct updated version in the version label. 4. The UI shows a status message before exit indicating the app will restart.
Comments: Windows 11 user-reported. BUG-073 BUG-074. All WPs Done.

Title: Bundled Python for Security Gate Execution
As a: a developer who does not have Python installed
I want: the launcher to provide its own Python runtime for the security gate
So that: created workspaces are secure out of the box without requiring any prerequisites
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The installer bundles the Python Embeddable Distribution (~15MB). 2. A ts-python shim is installed at a well-known fixed location and added to PATH. 3. The shim reads from a config file (python-path.txt) to locate the actual Python executable. 4. Created workspaces use ts-python in require-approval.json instead of bare python. 5. Reinstalling or moving the launcher updates python-path.txt — all existing workspaces continue working. 6. A "Relocate Python" option in the GUI allows updating the config after manual moves. 7. The launcher verifies ts-python accessibility before creating a workspace (fail-fast with helpful error).

Title: Provide an Agent Rules Documentation Template
I want: a project template that includes a complete AGENT-RULES.md and directs agents to read it first
So that: every generated workspace starts with clear, consistent agent permissions and operating rules
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The coding template includes AGENT-RULES.md with {{PROJECT_NAME}} placeholders. 2. AGENT-RULES.md documents the allowed zone, denied zones, full tool permission matrix, terminal rules, git rules, session-scoped denial counter behavior, and known workarounds. 3. copilot-instructions.md directs agents to read AGENT-RULES.md before starting work. 4. Project creation replaces AGENT-RULES.md placeholders with the actual project values.
Comments: v3.2 - Reconstructed from linked workpackages.

Title: Track Security Gate Denials Per Session
I want: the security gate to count denied actions per session with configurable lockout behavior and a reset mechanism
So that: repeated policy violations are contained without affecting future sessions unnecessarily
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The security gate stores denial counts in a session-scoped state file such as .hook_state.json. 2. Denial responses show progressive messaging such as "Block N of M". 3. When the configured threshold is reached the session is locked out from further blocked attempts. 4. Workspace configuration can enable or disable the counter and set the lockout threshold, with a safe default of 20. 5. A reset_hook_counter.py script clears the stored session counter state. 6. The design is backed by documented handling for session identification, multi-agent coordination, and audit logging decisions.
Comments: v3.2 - Reconstructed from linked workpackages.

Title: Allow Memory and LSP Tools in the Project Folder
I want: memory, directory creation, and VS Code symbol tools to work when they target the project folder
So that: agents can organize context and perform code navigation or refactoring without leaving the allowed zone
Acceptance Criteria: 1. create_directory is allowed when the target path is inside the project folder. 2. Memory tool operations scoped to project-folder content are allowed. 3. vscode_listCodeUsages is allowed for files in the project folder. 4. vscode_renameSymbol is allowed for files in the project folder. 5. Requests that target anything outside the project folder remain blocked.
Comments: v3.2 - Reconstructed from linked workpackages.

Title: Expand the Allowed Terminal Command Set
I want: more safe terminal commands to be available inside the project folder
So that: agents can complete common development tasks without unnecessary security denials
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Read-only commands including more, less, diff, fc, comp, sort, uniq, awk, and sed are allowed when their effective targets stay within the project folder. 2. Shell utility commands including touch, chmod, and ln are allowed when their effective targets stay within the project folder. 3. The git command allowlist is expanded for approved workflows needed inside the project folder. 4. Destructive or bypass-prone git operations remain blocked. 5. Docker and container support feasibility is researched and captured for future implementation decisions.
Comments: v3.2 - Reconstructed from linked workpackages.

Title: Limit Search Tools to the Project Folder
I want: all supported search tools scoped strictly to the project folder
So that: agents can find relevant project information without discovering files outside the allowed zone
Acceptance Criteria: 1. file_search returns results from the project folder only. 2. semantic_search is scoped to the project folder only. 3. search_subagent is scoped to the project folder only. 4. grep_search enforces project-folder scoping and respects search.exclude coverage. 5. Search results do not reveal files or matches outside the project folder.
Comments: v3.2 - Reconstructed from linked workpackages.

Title: Configure the Agent Block Counter in the Launcher GUI
I want: launcher controls for the denial counter so I can configure and reset it when creating or maintaining a workspace
So that: I can manage the security gate lockout behavior without editing configuration files manually
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The launcher GUI includes a counter threshold control and an enable or disable checkbox. 2. The selected counter settings are written into the workspace during project creation. 3. The launcher GUI includes a "Reset Agent Blocks" action. 4. Using the reset action clears the workspace's stored counter state.
Comments: v3.2 - Reconstructed from linked workpackages.

Title: Choose Whether README Files Are Included During Project Creation
I want: a launcher option to include or omit README files when creating a project and to remember my preference
So that: I can create a leaner workspace when I do not want template documentation copied in every time
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The launcher GUI includes an "Include README files" checkbox. 2. The checkbox state is persisted in OS-appropriate user settings and restored on later launches. 3. create_project accepts an include_readmes parameter. 4. When include_readmes is false README.md files are skipped during project creation, and when true the current behavior is preserved.
Comments: v3.2 - Reconstructed from linked workpackages.

Title: Windows Code Signing for Distribution
I want: the Windows installer and executable to be Authenticode-signed
So that: Windows SmartScreen does not flag the software as dangerous and users trust the download
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The .exe installer is signed with a valid Authenticode certificate; 2. Windows SmartScreen does not show "Windows protected your PC" warning for signed builds; 3. Signature is verifiable via signtool verify /pa; 4. Signing is automated in the CI/CD pipeline release.yml windows-build job; 5. Certificate renewal process is documented
Comments: Phase 1 - Windows code signing. macOS signing exists (US-025/026) but Windows has zero signing.

Title: Rename Templates to Product Presets
I want: to choose between "Agent Workbench" and "Certification Pipeline" presets when creating a workspace
So that: the template names clearly communicate their purpose and the product direction
Acceptance Criteria: 1. templates/coding/ is renamed to templates/agent-workbench/; 2. templates/creative-marketing/ is renamed to templates/certification-pipeline/; 3. GUI dropdown shows "Agent Workbench" (selectable) and "Certification Pipeline ...coming soon" (greyed out); 4. All internal references (code, tests, docs) updated to new names; 5. Certification Pipeline remains a stub (README.md only) marked as coming soon; 6. Project creation with Agent Workbench produces identical workspace to former Coding template
Comments: Phase 2 - Template renaming. Replaces "Coding" and "Creative / Marketing" naming.

Title: Programmer Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Programmer agent that writes and implements code
So that: I can delegate coding tasks to a focused implementation specialist
Acceptance Criteria: 1. programmer.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as a practical coder focused on implementation; 3. Agent tools include read, edit, search, execute; 4. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation; 5. Agent follows project AGENT-RULES.md conventions
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Programmer" vs Pipeline future formal "Developer".

Title: Brainstormer Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Brainstormer agent that generates creative solutions and explores approaches
So that: I can get diverse ideas before committing to an implementation
Acceptance Criteria: 1. brainstormer.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as a creative thinker that generates multiple approaches; 3. Agent tools include read and search (no edit - ideation only); 4. Agent explores trade-offs without premature commitment; 5. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Brainstormer" vs Pipeline future formal "Architect".

Title: Tester Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Tester agent that writes and runs tests
So that: my code is validated by a dedicated testing persona
Acceptance Criteria: 1. tester.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as quality-focused test writer; 3. Agent tools include read, edit, search, execute; 4. Agent writes unit and integration tests and finds edge cases; 5. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Tester" vs Pipeline future formal "Quality Engineer".

Title: Researcher Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Researcher agent that investigates technologies and solutions
So that: I can make informed technical decisions based on thorough investigation
Acceptance Criteria: 1. researcher.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as an investigator that evaluates and compares; 3. Agent tools include read, search, and fetch_webpage; 4. Agent produces structured summaries with pros and cons; 5. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Researcher" vs Pipeline future formal "Analyst".

Title: Scientist Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Scientist agent that approaches problems with analytical rigor
So that: I get data-driven insights and validated hypotheses
Acceptance Criteria: 1. scientist.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as analytical and hypothesis-driven; 3. Agent tools include read, edit, search, execute; 4. Agent runs benchmarks and experiments and documents findings with evidence; 5. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Scientist" vs Pipeline future formal "Data Engineer".

Title: Criticist Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Criticist agent that reviews code and finds problems
So that: I get honest constructive feedback before shipping
Acceptance Criteria: 1. criticist.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as a critical reviewer that identifies issues; 3. Agent tools include read and search only (does NOT fix - only identifies); 4. Agent finds bugs, security issues, and design flaws; 5. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Criticist" vs Pipeline future formal "Auditor".

Title: Planner Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Planner agent that breaks down tasks and organizes work
So that: complex projects are manageable with structured plans
Acceptance Criteria: 1. planner.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as an organizer that creates structured plans; 3. Agent tools include read and search (planning only - no implementation); 4. Agent identifies dependencies and produces actionable task lists; 5. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Planner" vs Pipeline future formal "Project Lead".

Title: Fixer Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Fixer agent specialized in debugging and troubleshooting
So that: bugs are efficiently diagnosed and resolved
Acceptance Criteria: 1. fixer.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as a debugger and root cause analyst; 3. Agent tools include read, edit, search, execute; 4. Agent reads errors, traces execution, and proposes and implements fixes; 5. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Fixer" vs Pipeline future formal "Diagnostician".

Title: Writer Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Writer agent that creates documentation
So that: my project has clear well-written docs without me writing them manually
Acceptance Criteria: 1. writer.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as a technical writer; 3. Agent tools include read, edit, search; 4. Agent creates READMEs, API docs, inline comments, and changelogs; 5. Agent adapts to project voice and structure; 6. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Writer" vs Pipeline future formal "Technical Writer".

Title: Prototyper Agent for Agent Workbench
I want: a Prototyper agent that rapidly builds proof-of-concept implementations
So that: I can validate ideas quickly before investing in production code
Acceptance Criteria: 1. prototyper.agent.md exists in templates/agent-workbench/.github/agents/; 2. Agent has a clear persona as speed-focused MVP builder; 3. Agent tools include read, edit, search, execute; 4. Agent builds quick-and-dirty implementations that validate feasibility; 5. Agent trades perfection for speed; 6. Agent is invokable in VS Code Copilot after workspace creation
Comments: Phase 3 - Agent Workbench agents. Casual name "Prototyper" (no Pipeline equivalent).

Title: Security Gate Must Match AGENT-RULES.md Permissions
I want: the security gate to enforce exactly the permissions documented in AGENT-RULES.md
So that: agents have the workspace root access, terminal access, memory access, and search capabilities the rulebook promises
Acceptance Criteria: 1. read_file and list_dir on workspace root succeed for top-level config files; 2. run_in_terminal is zone-checked not blanket-denied; 3. Git operations per AGENT-RULES succeed; 4. memory tool allows reading /memories/ and writing /memories/session/; 5. Search tool docs match actual behavior; 6. grep_search does not surface content read_file denies; 7. Automated tests in tests/SAF-046/ through tests/SAF-050/
Comments: v3.2.1 feedback report

Title: Fix Launcher GUI Branding and Project Type
I want: the launcher to display the correct application title and project type options
So that: the branding is consistent and I am not offered project types that do not exist
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Window title reads TS - Safe Agent Environment; 2. Coding project type removed; 3. Certification Pipeline Coming Soon appears disabled; 4. Only Agent Workbench selectable; 5. Disabled entry does not trigger creation
Comments: v3.2.1 feedback report

Title: Reliable Per-Conversation Denial Counter with Reset Fallback
As a: developer using the Safe Agent Environment
I want: the denial counter to reset automatically when I start a new conversation and have a working manual reset button
So that: my workspace does not become unusable over time due to accumulated blocks from past sessions
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Denial counter scopes blocks per-conversation; 2. New chat resets counter to zero; 3. Settings Reset Agent Blocks button visible and functional; 4. Reset clears all counters with confirmation; 5. Fallback session ID produces new ID per conversation
Comments: v3.2.1 feedback report

Title: Fix Coordinator Agent Name Resolution
As a: developer using the Coordinator agent mode
I want: the Coordinator delegation instructions to use the correct agent names
So that: the Coordinator can successfully delegate to all 10 specialist agents without name resolution failures
Acceptance Criteria: 1. All agent name references in coordinator.agent.md use PascalCase; 2. Delegation table uses corrected names; 3. agents frontmatter uses PascalCase; 4. All 10 delegations succeed on first attempt
Comments: v3.2.1 feedback report

Title: Open a Workspace Health Dashboard Window
As a: developer using the Agent Environment Launcher
I want: a dedicated Workspace Health Dashboard window where I can select a workspace, validate it, and access workspace health actions
So that: I can inspect and manage workspace security state from a single purpose-built UI instead of using Settings for operational tasks
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The main launcher window includes a Workspace Health Dashboard action that opens a separate CTkToplevel window rather than embedding the dashboard in the main page; 2. The dashboard window follows the existing launcher dialog styling conventions already used for the Settings window, including the established color constants and modal top-level behavior; 3. The dashboard allows the user to browse for or enter a workspace folder path; 4. When the selected path is not itself the workspace root, the dashboard checks one directory level above and one directory level below the selected folder to find a valid launcher-created workspace before rejecting the selection; 5. A workspace is treated as valid only when the expected launcher workspace structure can be resolved, including the hook scripts location needed for health data; 6. If validation succeeds through an adjusted parent or child folder, the dashboard clearly shows which folder was selected and which folder is being treated as the resolved workspace root; 7. If validation fails, the dashboard shows a non-crashing, actionable message and does not display stale statistics from a previous workspace; 8. The Reset Agent Blocks capability is removed from the Settings window and is available from the dashboard instead; 9. The dashboard includes a reset action for the resolved workspace that clears the workspace block state and confirms the result to the user; 10. Existing Settings capabilities unrelated to block management, including Python runtime relocation, remain available and functional
Comments: Assumes the current reset flow in the Settings dialog is migrated, not duplicated. Validation should resolve the workspace root without scanning recursively beyond one level up or down.

Title: Persist Extended Security Gate Session Statistics
I want: the security gate to persist richer per-session statistics for blocked activity and session totals
So that: the launcher dashboard can show meaningful workspace health information without storing unnecessary high-volume data
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The security gate extends the current per-session state model in .hook_state.json beyond deny_count, locked, and timestamp to support dashboard-readable statistics; 2. The persisted design records blocked-command details only for denied actions and does not persist full details for allowed tool calls; 3. The persisted design records per-session zone-violation statistics in a way that distinguishes violation categories relevant to the existing gate rules, such as denied-zone access versus other policy violations; 4. The security gate tracks a per-session total tool call count that increments for all evaluated tool calls but stores only the aggregate count rather than a log of allowed calls; 5. The implementation continues to identify sessions using the existing OTel JSONL method when available and the existing fallback session mechanism when telemetry is unavailable; 6. The persisted state remains backward-compatible with older .hook_state.json files that contain only the current fields, and older files are upgraded or defaulted safely without crashing the hook; 7. Corrupt, partial, or missing statistics data is handled gracefully by falling back to safe defaults rather than blocking normal launcher or hook operation; 8. Counter configuration in counter_config.json continues to control whether the denial counter is active and what threshold triggers session lockout; 9. The additional statistics collection does not change existing allow or deny decisions of the security gate; it only adds data capture needed for observability; 10. The implementation documents or defines the persisted schema clearly enough that the launcher dashboard can consume it without reverse-engineering hook internals
Comments: This story is intentionally the data-layer foundation for the dashboard. Persist blocked commands only when a denial occurs; allowed operations should contribute only to aggregate counters to keep data volume low.

Title: Display Real-Time Per-Session Workspace Health Statistics
I want: the Workspace Health Dashboard to show live workspace-level and session-level security statistics
So that: I can understand how each chat session is interacting with the workspace security boundaries while work is ongoing
Acceptance Criteria: 1. After a valid workspace is resolved, the dashboard reads and displays workspace health data from that workspace's hook telemetry and state files; 2. The dashboard shows an overall workspace summary and a per-session breakdown rather than only a single combined counter; 3. For each identified chat session, the dashboard displays at minimum the denial count, whether the session is locked, the total blocked commands recorded for that session, the zone-violation statistics recorded for that session, and the total tool call count for that session; 4. The session list is presented in chronological order based on the best available session timestamp or first-seen information from the underlying data; 5. When a human-readable VS Code chat session name can be extracted from available telemetry or metadata, the dashboard shows that name; 6. When no reliable session name is obtainable, the dashboard falls back to deterministic labels in chronological order such as Session #1 through Session #n; 7. The dashboard updates automatically while the selected workspace remains open in the dashboard, without requiring the user to reopen the window or manually reload after every hook event; 8. Real-time refresh handles new sessions, updated counts, and newly locked sessions without duplicating rows or losing already displayed sessions; 9. If telemetry files or state files are temporarily missing, incomplete, or being written concurrently, the dashboard degrades gracefully by showing the latest valid data or an explicit unavailable state instead of crashing; 10. The displayed statistics reflect the data minimization rule: blocked command details are shown only for denied actions, while allowed activity is represented only through aggregate totals such as total tool call count
Comments: Assumes live refresh is implemented by polling or a comparable safe desktop-app mechanism. Session naming should prefer extracted names only when the source is reliable; otherwise chronological numbering is the required fallback.

Title: Manage Session Blocks from the Workspace Health Dashboard
As a: developer maintaining a workspace after blocked agent activity
I want: to delete blocked-command data and reset block counts for a single session or for all sessions directly from the dashboard
So that: I can recover from stale or unwanted block history without editing hook files manually
Acceptance Criteria: 1. The dashboard provides an action to clear block-related data for an individual session selected in the session list; 2. The dashboard provides a separate action to clear block-related data for all sessions in the resolved workspace; 3. A per-session clear operation removes that session's persisted block data and any associated blocked-command details without affecting other sessions; 4. A clear-all operation removes all persisted session block data for the workspace and leaves the state in a valid, reusable format; 5. When block data is deleted for a session, the dashboard updates to reflect the new state without requiring the window to be reopened; 6. When all block data is deleted, the workspace summary and session list refresh immediately to the cleared state; 7. Destructive actions require an explicit user confirmation step so block history is not removed accidentally; 8. The migrated Reset Agent Blocks function in the dashboard remains available as the workspace-wide recovery action and is consistent with the clear-all behavior defined for this feature; 9. If the target state file is missing or corrupt, the dashboard handles the operation gracefully and informs the user of the actual outcome; 10. Block-management actions operate only on the currently resolved workspace and never on another folder that happened to be selected before validation
Comments: This story covers operational cleanup and recovery only. It should use the same resolved-workspace logic as the dashboard validation flow so deletes cannot target the wrong directory after a misclick.

Title: Whitelist Agent-Facing .github/ Subdirectories Read-Only
As a: developer using an AI agent in the Safe Agent Environment
I want: the agent to access its own configuration files (agents, skills, prompts, instructions) without wasting denial blocks
So that: agent capabilities work as designed and no blocks are consumed on system-directed reads
Acceptance Criteria: 1. read_file on .github/agents/, .github/skills/, .github/prompts/, .github/instructions/ succeeds (read-only); 2. list_dir on those directories succeeds; 3. create_file / edit_file / replace_string_in_file targeting any .github/ path is still denied; 4. .github/hooks/ (security gate code) remains fully denied for read and write; 5. .github/ remains hidden in files.exclude - agents discover these paths only via system prompts, not via browsing; 6. Block 1 is no longer consumed reading copilot-instructions.md

Title: Close Zone Classification Bypass via .git Directory
I want: .git directories to never be selected as the project folder
So that: git init at the workspace root cannot break zone classification and lock the agent out of the real project folder
Acceptance Criteria: 1. .git is in _DENY_DIRS in zone_classifier.py; 2. Other common VCS dirs (.hg, .svn) are also excluded defensively; 3. detect_project_folder() skips all dot-prefixed directories; 4. After git init at workspace root, the actual project folder remains accessible; 5. Paths inside .git/ are classified as deny

Title: Close get_changed_files Zone Enforcement Bypass
I want: get_changed_files to not expose files from denied zones
So that: zone enforcement cannot be bypassed by a single tool call when a git repo exists
Acceptance Criteria: 1. get_changed_files is moved from _ALWAYS_ALLOW_TOOLS to a conditional check; 2. When .git/ is at workspace root (outside project folder), the tool is denied; 3. When .git/ is inside the project folder, the tool is allowed; 4. No file paths or contents from denied zones are exposed via the tool output

Title: Fix Terminal Command Filtering Inconsistencies
As a: developer using terminal commands in the Safe Agent Environment
I want: the terminal filter to correctly handle PowerShell cmdlets, parameters, and property-access patterns
So that: standard PowerShell workflows do not waste denial blocks on false positives
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Remove-Item <project-path> is allowed (same as del); 2. dir -Name is allowed (valid PS parameter, not a security risk); 3. (Get-Content file).Count and similar property-access patterns are allowed; 4. Test-Path is added to the command allowlist (read-only diagnostic); 5. cmd /c remains blocked (legitimate security concern); 6. Existing tests for all terminal categories still pass

Title: Make NoAgentZone Visible in VS Code Explorer
I want: to see the NoAgentZone folder in the VS Code file explorer
So that: I can easily manage its contents without needing the terminal
Acceptance Criteria: 1. NoAgentZone is removed from files.exclude in .vscode/settings.json; 2. NoAgentZone remains in search.exclude (agent searches do not index it); 3. Security gate zone enforcement still denies all agent access to NoAgentZone

Title: macOS Source Distribution with Git-Based Updates
I want: to install the launcher from source via pip install and receive updates via git
So that: I can use the launcher on macOS without PyInstaller bundling, code signing, or Gatekeeper issues
Acceptance Criteria: 1. A documented make install-macos (or equivalent shell script) installs the launcher from the cloned repo into a virtualenv; 2. Running agent-launcher from the virtualenv launches the GUI without PyInstaller; 3. The ts-python shim is deployed correctly for source-mode installs (points to the virtualenv Python, not a bundled Python.framework); 4. An update command or script pulls latest changes via git and re-installs; 5. The updater in core/updater.py detects source-mode and offers git-based updates instead of downloading DMG assets; 6. The macOS installation guide is updated with the source installation as the primary method, with DMG as an alternative; 7. Works on both Apple Silicon (arm64) and Intel (x86_64) Macs with Python 3.11+

Title: Fail-Closed Security Gate When Python Runtime Unavailable
I want: the security gate to block ALL tool calls and inform me when the Python runtime is unavailable or misconfigured
So that: the security model cannot be silently bypassed when the runtime path is lost, corrupted, or the launcher is closed
Acceptance Criteria: 1. When python-path.txt is missing, empty, or points to a non-existent executable, the ts-python shim outputs a valid JSON deny response to stdout that blocks the tool call in VS Code. 2. The deny message informs the agent and user that the security gate cannot run and all tool calls are temporarily blocked. 3. The security gate works correctly regardless of whether the launcher GUI is open or closed. 4. After a version update via the GUI, python-path.txt always contains the correct path to the new installation python-embed/python.exe. 5. The workspace root README.md is present in newly created workspaces. 6. No tool call can silently pass through when the security gate is non-functional.
Comments: v3.2.4 feedback report revealed critical fail-open behavior. Covers BUG-156, BUG-157, BUG-158.

Title: v3.2.6 Documentation Alignment and Gate Bug Fixes
I want: AGENT-RULES and copilot-instructions to accurately describe the actual security gate behavior and the Test-Path bug on dot-prefixed root paths to be fixed
So that: there are no discrepancies between documentation and implementation and agents operate without unnecessary denials or confusion
Acceptance Criteria: 1. AGENT-RULES accurately documents .github/ partial read access model (read_file allowed and list_dir denied). 2. copilot-instructions Known Tool Limitations table no longer claims memory is blocked. 3. Test-Path on workspace root dot-prefixed paths (e.g. .venv) works without denial. 4. cmd /c blocking is documented as intentional in AGENT-RULES. 5. AGENT-RULES is minimal with no ambiguity. 6. Workspace root README.md exists for human users. 7. Developer-only agents/README.md removed from template.
Comments: v3.2.6 release - addresses all v3.2.5 feedback report findings

Title: Automate Release Version Management
I want: a single release command that bumps all version strings, creates the release commit and annotated tag, pushes them, and is backed by CI validation that checks version consistency against the git tag
So that: version string mismatches cannot reach a release build and the manual error-prone tagging workflow is eliminated
Acceptance Criteria: 1. scripts/release.py <version> updates version in all 5 files: config.py, pyproject.toml, setup.iss, build_dmg.sh, build_appimage.sh. 2. The script performs updates atomically and aborts without partial state if any file cannot be updated. 3. The script creates a commit with standardized message format. 4. The script creates an annotated git tag and pushes both commit and tag. 5. A validate-version CI job in release.yml extracts tag version and asserts all 5 files match. 6. CI fails before release publication if any version string differs. 7. BUG-164 (build_appimage.sh stale version) is resolved. 8. Orchestrator agent instructions use scripts/release.py instead of manual git tag commands. 9. A documented release run produces a tagged release where all files report the same version.
Comments: Prevents recurrence of BUG-163 and BUG-164

Title: Agent Workbench AgentDocs Central Documentation System
As a: developer using the Agent Workbench template
I want: a pre-seeded AgentDocs folder that serves as the single source of truth for all agent-generated documentation with a clear philosophy of living documents over sprawl
So that: all agents and humans share one lightweight knowledge base that stays current without creating documentation overhead
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Project/AgentDocs/ folder exists in the template with README.md and 5 standard documents (architecture.md, decisions.md, research-log.md, progress.md, open-questions.md); 2. README.md explains the 5-pillar philosophy (AgentDocs is the brain; living documents; speed over ceremony; read before act; leave traces); 3. AGENT-RULES.md includes an AgentDocs section requiring agents to read progress.md at session start and update relevant docs before finishing; 4. copilot-instructions.md and workspace README.md reference the AgentDocs folder in the workspace structure; 5. Standard documents contain minimal placeholder structure ready for agent use
Comments: Part of agent-workbench template redesign for quick dev/research/testing workflows

Title: Agent Workbench Agent and Prompt Redesign
As a: developer using the Agent Workbench template
I want: a streamlined set of 6 specialist agents and 3 structured prompts all aligned with the AgentDocs philosophy and tuned for rapid development and research
So that: the agent roster matches my workflow without certification overhead and every agent contributes to and reads from the shared AgentDocs knowledge base
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Exactly 6 agents exist: Coordinator, Brainstormer, Researcher, Planner, Programmer, Tester; 2. All references to removed agents (Scientist, Criticist, Writer, Fixer, Prototyper) are eliminated; 3. Each agent reads from and writes to specific AgentDocs documents as part of its workflow; 4. Coordinator produces a working demonstrator/artifact as its terminal goal; 5. Researcher has fetch tool and mandates sourced links written to research-log.md; 6. 3 structured prompts created: Critical Review, Prototype, Root Cause Analysis; 7. Each prompt follows a phased workflow with defined output format; 8. All agents reflect speed-over-ceremony philosophy
Comments: Part of agent-workbench template redesign for quick dev/research/testing workflows

Title: Agent Workbench Skills and Tidyup Agent
As a: developer using the Agent Workbench template
I want: two practical skills (AgentDocs writing and safety-critical development) and a Tidyup agent that verifies documentation matches the actual project state
So that: agents produce consistent AgentDocs entries, apply safety guardrails when needed, and documentation never drifts from reality
Acceptance Criteria: 1. agentdocs-update skill exists teaching proper AgentDocs entry format and cross-referencing; 2. safety-critical skill exists with hazard-first checklist for safety-relevant tasks; 3. Old ts-code-review skill is removed; 4. Tidyup agent exists with read+search+edit+execute tools; 5. Tidyup agent checks AgentDocs vs actual code/project state and fixes drift; 6. Coordinator agents list includes Tidyup; 7. All agents reference applicable skills in their descriptions
Comments: Part of agent-workbench v3.3.0 release

Title: Agent Workbench Template Restructure and Prefix Rename
As a: developer using the Agent Workbench template
I want: AGENT-RULES.md to be the central document inside AgentDocs/ combining all agent rules and documentation philosophy, copilot-instructions to be a slim pointer, the Project/README.md removed, and the workspace prefix shortened from TS-SAE- to SAE-
So that: agents find all rules in one authoritative location inside AgentDocs/, copilot-instructions are compact, and workspace folder names are shorter while still clearly identifying secured workspaces
Acceptance Criteria: 1. AgentDocs/AGENT-RULES.md exists containing merged content from previous AGENT-RULES.md plus AgentDocs README philosophy and standard documents table; 2. Project-level AGENT-RULES.md is removed; 3. Project/README.md is removed; 4. AgentDocs/README.md is replaced by AgentDocs/AGENT-RULES.md; 5. copilot-instructions.md is a compact philosophy statement pointing to AgentDocs/AGENT-RULES.md for full rules; 6. All agent.md files reference AgentDocs/AGENT-RULES.md instead of AGENT-RULES.md; 7. Root README.md references AgentDocs/AGENT-RULES.md; 8. Workspace prefix is SAE- instead of TS-SAE- in project_creator.py and app.py; 9. All placeholder replacements use SAE- prefix; 10. All affected tests updated and passing
Comments: v3.3.6 — Template restructure and prefix rename

Title: Document Coordinator Agent Model Configuration
As a: developer using the Agent Workbench template
I want: the Coordinator agent to be configured with Claude Opus 4.6 as primary model and Claude Sonnet 4.6 as fallback in the committed template
So that: the coordinator uses the most capable model available with graceful fallback and this configuration is tracked in the project
Acceptance Criteria: 1. coordinator.agent.md model field is ['Claude Opus 4.6 (copilot)', 'Claude Sonnet 4.6 (copilot)']; 2. The change is committed and tracked via a workpackage; 3. DOC-041 (existing open WP for agent model updates) is superseded or fulfilled
Comments: v3.3.6 — Makes locally applied coordinator model change persistent

Title: Harden Terminal Hook Against Path Bypass and Env Exfiltration
I want: the terminal security gate to correctly detect and block unquoted Windows paths and environment variable exfiltration attempts
So that: no agent can bypass the security gate by omitting quotes around paths or using dollar-env references in unrestricted commands
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Unquoted Windows paths (e.g. Get-Content C:\\Users\\angel\\secret.txt) are denied when outside the project folder; 2. Quoted Windows paths continue to be denied as before; 3. dollar-env: references in any command (including write-output echo write-host) are denied; 4. NoAgentZone is inaccessible via any terminal path form (quoted or unquoted); 5. Regression tests cover all 10 bypass scenarios from the security-hook-report; 6. Integrity hashes updated after changes
Comments: Addresses BUG-173 BUG-174. Root cause fixes in security_gate.py _tokenize_segment() and _validate_args().

Title: Deny-Event Audit Log for Security Gate
I want: a persistent audit log of all denied tool calls written by the security gate
So that: I can review what restricted actions agents attempted even after the chat session ends
Acceptance Criteria: 1. Every deny decision appends a JSON line to .github/hooks/scripts/audit.jsonl; 2. Each entry contains timestamp session_id tool_name decision reason and sanitized target; 3. The audit log is append-only and never truncated by the security gate; 4. Audit log file is inside the denied zone so agents cannot read or modify it; 5. No sensitive data (full file contents or credentials) is written to the log
Comments: Addresses BUG-175. Provides user visibility into blocked agent actions.

Title: Harden Bash and PowerShell Fallback Hooks
I want: the bash and PowerShell fallback hook scripts to be hardened against the same bypass vectors found in the Python security gate
So that: workspaces where the Python gate is unavailable still have effective terminal command filtering
Acceptance Criteria: 1. require-approval.sh blocks unquoted Unix paths outside the project folder; 2. require-approval.ps1 blocks unquoted Windows paths outside the project folder; 3. Both scripts block dollar-variable environment exfiltration; 4. Both scripts block command substitution and eval-based obfuscation; 5. Regression tests cover key bypass vectors for each script; 6. Fix parity maintained between Python gate bash and PS1 variants
Comments: Addresses BUG-176. Parallel hardening of shell fallback hooks.

Title: Fix Stale Test Suite to Unblock CI/CD Releases
I want: all stale test assertions updated to reflect the current codebase state and the regression baseline reset to zero known failures
So that: the CI test gate passes on Windows, macOS, and Linux and releases can be built and shipped
Acceptance Criteria: 1. All DOC-category tests pass with assertions updated for the current .agent.md frontmatter and content used by the redesigned Agent Workbench agents. 2. All tests that currently hardcode obsolete launcher versions are updated to use dynamic version sourcing or the current expected version 3.3.11. 3. All tests with stale template-path or workspace-prefix expectations are updated to reference templates/agent-workbench/ and the SAE- workspace prefix. 4. All CI, codesign, and security-related tests pass using the current expected values and behaviors. 5. All JSONL-migration tests pass using the current file formats and repository conventions. 6. tests/regression-baseline.json is updated so the known-failure count is zero (_count: 0). 7. CI test gate passes on Windows, macOS, and Linux for both test.yml and release.yml. 8. testing-protocol.md is updated to require test assertion updates alongside code refactors. 9. ADR-008 is proposed documenting the tests-track-code process rule.
Comments: Blocking all releases. Root cause: 4 waves of codebase evolution broke test assertions without updating tests.
